subject: [PATCH v5 06/22] coco/tdx-host: Expose P-SEAMLDR information via sysfs

reviews.author: Chao Gao
reviews.summary: Reviewer suggested removing revision and chapter numbers from sysfs output as they may make the output too verbose, but noted that having these references is a nice-to-have feature
reviews.raw_body:
I think it's nice-to-have. Without specific references, people would need to
read the entire 26-page spec. But I can drop them if they make it too verbose.

reviews.author: Xiaoyao Li
reviews.summary: reviewer noted that the 'revision' attribute may not be useful for finding an old version, as it only indicates what the attribute was built against
reviews.raw_body:
I have some concerns about the "revision". The good thing is that it can 
tell what the attribute was built against while the bad thing is that it 
might not be easy for people to find an old revision years later.

reviews.author: Chao Gao
reviews.summary: Reviewer Chao Gao noted that dropping revision numbers is acceptable, as long as chapter titles are kept for targeted references.
reviews.raw_body:
I'm okay with dropping the revision number.

The intent is not to require readers to find the exact same revision. If they
can find that exact revision, great. If not, the chapter numbers may differ
in newer revisions.

Kirill, do you mean dropping the numbers but keeping the chapter titles:

		See Intel Trust Domain Extensions - SEAM Loader (SEAMLDR)
		Interface Specification, Chapter "SEAMLDR_INFO" and Chapter
		"SEAMLDR.INSTALL" for more information.

This keeps a targeted reference so readers do not need to scan the entire spec.

2026-03-18.patch_summary: This patch corrects an issue in the x86/virt/seamldr module where it incorrectly handles memory alignment for a signature (@sig) that is not 4k aligned. The fix involves adjusting the pointer to the next 4k boundary when handling such cases. Additionally, the patch adds a comment to clarify how block->offset_of_module is calculated relative to the start of the struct tdx_blob.
